     The Numbers Business Report

Country Strong (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,218,921Details
International Box Office $383,066
Worldwide Box Office $20,601,987
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,904,701
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,416,053
Total $11,320,754
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A rising young singer-songwriter, Beau Hutton, gets involved with fallen country music star Kelly Canter and the pair embark on a tour to resurrect her career, helmed by her husband/manager, James, who accompanies them along with beauty queen-turned-singer Chiles Stanton. Complications arising from romantic entanglements and old demons threaten to derail them all.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$30,452 (0.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.76 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:98.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2 opening theaters/1,441 max. theaters, 4.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $30,495,022

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 22nd, 2010 (Limited) by Sony Pictures
January 7th, 2011 (Expands Wide) by Sony Pictures
Video Release: April 12th, 2011 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic elements involving alcohol abuse and some sexual content.
(Rating bulletin 2145, 11/3/2010)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Plot point: Addiction, Comeback, Infidelity, Musicians, Off the Wagon / Relapse, Rehab / Former Addict, Suicide
Social setting: Country and Western Music, Music Industry, On Tour
Subgenre: Life Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Material Pictures, Screen Gems
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
